role_title: COO - Program Manager, Vice President / SSIM

level_signals: Manages subject matter experts and project managers supporting workstreams
Compensation range $110,000–$188,750 consistent with VP-level at a large financial institution

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Project management experience on large complex programs
Initiative and the ability to work independently or with a team
Strong sense of urgency when performing tasks and projects; ability to respond to shifting priorities
Ability to deliver professional documents - strong knowledge of "off the shelf" (PowerPoint, Excel, Word ).
Undergraduate degree – with demonstrated interest in Business, Finance or Computer Science

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Working knowledge of MS Project, Jira and Clarity preferred.

scope.surface: Investment Management COO – Strategic Program Management across Product, Platform, and Services pillars
scope.business_model: B2B institutional investment management and asset servicing
primary_metrics_inferred: Achievement of IM Objective Key Results (OKRs)
On-time and on-scope delivery of strategic initiatives
Program budget and resource tracking accuracy
core_responsibilities: Be the primary face-off with the COO Investment Management Product, Platform and Services pillars and the IM CAO Strategic Program Management organization to deliver Strategic initiatives and drive to achieving IM Objective Key Results (OKRs).
Understand/document requirements including the products being used in IM organizations to determine how they can be leveraged or eliminated.

Drive the joint and internal governance with all stakeholders. This includes the development and maintenance of project plans, status reports, issue and action logs and scope and requirements documents.

Drive closure on all aspects of the program with support from the functional teams and SME's, including: contracts and commercials, scope and solution, project sequencing and planning, development, testing, conversion and post conversion activities.
Manage subject matter experts and project managers supporting workstreams to deliver the end-to-end solution.

Lead and support governance meetings - set meeting agendas and document meeting minutes for meetings to discuss progress and address issues to achieve milestones and objectives for the execution of the plans
Track resources, hours and hiring for resources, and rolling up globally with inputs from Global teams. Compile resource plans and program budgets.
Actively communicate the status of work with other team members to ensure that everyone involved is fully informed.

green_flags: Clear scope of responsibilities across a defined COO organizational structure
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
VP-level title signals seniority and organizational standing
red_flags: No minimum years of experience stated, making seniority expectations harder to calibrate
Location appears onsite (Boston, MA) but no explicit remote/hybrid policy stated in JD body
Broad compensation band ($78,750 spread) suggests significant variability based on experience level

scope.business_model (inferred from company description and IM COO context)

ambiguities: No explicit remote or hybrid language in the JD body; location is inferred as onsite from the URL path 'Boston-Massachusetts' and absence of any flexibility language.

Undergraduate degree requirement uses 'with demonstrated interest in Business, Finance or Computer Science' which is ambiguous — could mean a degree in those fields or simply coursework/exposure. Treated as a general undergraduate degree requirement.

Several 'What we value' bullets are framed as skills that 'will help you succeed' rather than being labeled Required or Preferred, creating ambiguity. Treated as hard requirements given unconditional framing, with the exception of tool preferences explicitly labeled 'preferred'.
